forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system east of Tasmania will maintain a ridge over SA this week. A cold front will pass over the south on Saturday, weakening and clearing in the east early Sunday. A new high pressure system will develop in the Bight on Sunday, slowly drifting to the southeast on Monday while continuing to extend a ridge over SA.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.
Dry. Cloud increasing across the west. Maximum temperatures above average, near average in the northeast. Northeast to northerly winds, freshening about the west during the day.
Dry with thin high cloud developing from the west. Moderate east to northeasterly winds.

Showers developing about western and southern coastal districts, extending to remaining parts of the agricultural in the evening. Possible isolated thunderstorms about the southern agricultural areas and West Coast. Maximum temperatures above average. Northeast to northwesterly winds freshening ahead of a south to southwesterly change moving over the west and south later.

Showers about the agricultural areas during the morning, clearing to the east during the day. Isolated morning fog patches in the west. Maximum temperatures near average. Southwest to southeasterly winds, fresh at times near the coasts.

text.p: Light showers about southern coastal fringes, otherwise dry. Possible isolated morning thunderstorms about central and southern areas. Isolated morning fog patches about the agricultural areas. Maximum temperatures slightly below average. South to southeasterly winds with afternoon seabreezes.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: dry in the far north with generally less than 2 mm across the southern pastorals, West Coast and northern agricultural areas. Up to 2-5mm across the Peninsulas and Kangaroo Island with localised rainfall totals of 10 mm with isolated thunderstorms.
